html:
The proposal suggests changes to the abortion law (1974:595). The purpose of these changes is to modernize the language of the law and to adapt it to medical advancements and the needs of pregnant women.

It should be clarified that a woman who is pregnant may have an abortion until the end of the 18th week of pregnancy.

Abortion and termination of pregnancy should be provided within the framework of a general hospital or another healthcare facility specifically adapted for this purpose and approved by the Swedish Inspectorate for Healthcare and Welfare. Abortion should be provided promptly.

Home abortions should be made possible by removing the requirement that abortions or terminations of pregnancy must be performed in a general hospital or another healthcare facility. The proposal also opens up the possibility for more flexible medication management and remote care. By removing the requirement that only those authorized to practice medicine can perform abortions, it becomes possible for, among others, midwives to independently perform medical abortions.

The proposed changes to the law are scheduled to come into effect on January 1, 2027. A healthcare facility that has been approved by the Swedish Inspectorate for Healthcare and Welfare before the law comes into effect may continue to operate without a new approval from the Inspectorate for Healthcare and Welfare.

Proposed Parliamentary Decision

The government's proposal: The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the abortion law (1974:595). 4
tldr: The government wants to amend the law on abortion to make it more modern. The proposal includes allowing abortions to be performed at home, and giving midwives more responsibility, with the aim of these changes taking effect from January 1, 2027.
lattlast:
The government has proposed new regulations for abortion. The purpose is to update the law to align with current medical technology and the needs of pregnant women. A key aspect is to clearly state that a woman has the right to have an abortion up to week 18 of pregnancy.

A significant change is the possibility of performing abortions at home. Previously, there were requirements that it must be done in a hospital or a specialized clinic. Now, the aim is to remove this requirement to make healthcare more flexible. The intention is also to use more medications and provide care remotely, for example, via the internet.

The proposal also means that more people can assist with an abortion. Currently, only doctors are allowed to perform abortions, but with the new regulations, midwives will also be able to perform medical abortions independently. This is to make healthcare faster and more accessible.

If the parliament approves the proposal, the new regulations will come into effect on January 1, 2027. Hospitals and clinics that have already received approval from the Swedish Inspectorate for Healthcare and Biotechnology (the agency that oversees healthcare) will not need to apply for a new approval when the new regulations come into effect.
